---
title: "Figure 1"
author: "Mei-Ju May Chen (mjmchen[at]ntu.edu.tw)"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: flatly
    self_contained: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo       = TRUE,
  message    = FALSE,
  warning    = FALSE,
  fig.align  = "center",
  fig.width  = 8,
  fig.height = 6
)
```

## Load libraries

```{r libraries}
library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)  
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(openxlsx)
library(ggpubr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(maftools)
library(forestploter)
```

## Color codes

```{r color-codes}
# Plot the features and scatterplot with correlation and p-value
major_celltype_color = c("#FC4E07","#E7B800","#00AFBB",brewer.pal(n = 9, name = "Set1")[c(4:5,7,2,8:9)], "lightblue")
minor_celltype_color = c(brewer.pal(n=5, "Set2"), "#E7B800", brewer.pal(n=4, "Set1")) 
```

## Load data

```{r load-data}
rds_path <- "data-to-path"
ob <- readRDS(rds_path)
# Quick overview
ob
```


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Figure 1C

```{r fig1c, fig.cap="Figure 1C"}
labels = rev(levels(factor(as.vector(ob$Cell.Type.L2))))
ob$Cell.Type.L2 = factor(ob$Cell.Type.L2, levels = labels[c(1:2,6,3:5,7:10)])
DimPlot(ob, group.by = "Cell.Type.L2") + scale_color_manual(values = major_celltype_color) +
  DimPlot(ob, group.by = "donor")  
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Figure 1D

```{r fig1d, fig.cap="Figure 1D"}
ob_disease <- SplitObject(ob, split.by = "Sample.type")
features = c("RPS4Y1", "EIF1AY", "USP9Y",  "UTY",    "KDM5D",  "RPS4X",  "USP9X",  "EIF1AX", "KDM6A",  "KDM5C",  "RPLP0")
a = rev(levels(ob$Sample.type))[1]
#for(a in rev(levels(ob$Sample.type))){
  ob_list = list()
  ob_list <- SplitObject(ob_disease[[a]], split.by = "donor")
  dotplots <- list()
  orderlist = names(ob_list)
  orderlist = rev(orderlist)
  # Iterate through each split object and create a DotPlot
  dotplots=list()
  for (i in orderlist) {
    # Create DotPlot for the current split object
    # Create DotPlot for the current split object
    # Add cell counts to Cell.Type labels
    ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2
    cell_counts <- ob_list[[i]]@meta.data %>%
      group_by(Cell.Type.L2) %>%
      summarise(count = n()) %>%
      mutate(Cell.Type.L2 = paste0(Cell.Type.L2, " (N=", count, ")"))
    
    # Update the Cell.Type factor levels in the metadata
    levels(ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2) = cell_counts$Cell.Type.L2
    ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2 = factor(ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2, levels = rev(levels(ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2)))
    # Create DotPlot with updated Cell.Type labels
    p <- DotPlot(ob_list[[i]], features = features, group.by = "Cell.Type.L2", scale = F, 
                    col.min = 0, dot.min = 0) +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8)
      )       
    
    # Store the plot in the list
    dotplots[[i]] <- p
  }
  # Combine all DotPlots into one plot layout
  combined_plot <- wrap_plots(dotplots, nrow=4)
  # Save the combined plot as a PDF
  #pdf(file = file.path(resPath, paste0("F22_DotPlot_", a, ".pdf")), width = 8, height = 12)
  print(combined_plot)
  #dev.off()
#}

```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Figure 1E

```{r fig1e, fig.cap="Figure 1E"}
ob_loy = SplitObject(ob, split.by = "LOY.status")
x_limits <- c(-0.4,0.9)
# Save the RidgePlot for ROY as a PDF
# pdf(file = file.path(resPath, "F23_RidgePlot_Y_Score1_ROY.pdf"), width = 12, height = 5)
RidgePlot(ob_loy[["ROY"]], features = "Y.score", group.by = "Cell.Type.L2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = alpha(major_celltype_color, 0.5)) +
  xlim(x_limits) + 
  NoLegend()
# dev.off()

# Save the RidgePlot for LOY as a PDF
#pdf(file = file.path(resPath, "F24_RidgePlot_Y_Score1_LOY.pdf"), width = 12, height = 5)
RidgePlot(ob_loy[["LOY"]], features = "Y.score", group.by = "Cell.Type.L2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = alpha(major_celltype_color, 0.5)) +
  xlim(x_limits) + 
  NoLegend()
#dev.off()

```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Figure 1F

```{r fig1f, fig.cap="oncoplot"}
# Paths (point these to the TCGA / DKFZ multi-omics directory)
dataPath <- "data-to-path"
resPath  <- "data-to-path"

cancer <- "LUAD"

# `kdm` comes from gene_info.rda;
# `male.maf` (TCGA + DKFZ male MAF, 99 genes, hg38) comes from the second .rda.
load(file.path(resPath, "gene_info.rda"))
load(file.path(resPath, "male.maf.tcga.dkfz_99genes_combined.hg38.rda"))

# Driver genes (IntOGen) and the combined gene list used downstream
driver_genes = read.csv(file.path(dataPath, paste0("IntOGen-DriverGenes_", cancer, ".tsv")), sep = "\t")
driver_genes = driver_genes[order(driver_genes$Samples...., decreasing = T),]
combined_genes = driver_genes$Symbol[driver_genes$Cohorts>=1]

# Y status (LOY / ROY) per sample, restricted to the cancer of interest
y_status <- read.csv(file.path(dataPath, "TCGA_Y_status_lung.csv"),
                     header = TRUE, row.names = 1)
y_status <- y_status[y_status$Cohort == cancer, ]

# Split MAF by major Y status
loy.maf <- subsetMaf(male.maf,
                     tsb = male.maf@clinical.data$Tumor_Sample_Barcode[
                       male.maf@clinical.data$Major_Y_status == "LOY"])
roy.maf <- subsetMaf(male.maf,
                     tsb = male.maf@clinical.data$Tumor_Sample_Barcode[
                       male.maf@clinical.data$Major_Y_status == "ROY"])

# Differential mutation test LOY vs ROY
pt.vs.rt <- mafCompare(
  loy.maf, roy.maf, useCNV = FALSE,
  m1Name = paste0(cancer, "-", paste(names(table(loy.maf@clinical.data$Y_status)), collapse = "/")),
  m2Name = paste0(cancer, "-", paste(names(table(roy.maf@clinical.data$Y_status)), collapse = "/")),
  minMut = 5
)

data <- pt.vs.rt$results[pt.vs.rt$results$pval < 0.1, ]
data <- data[data$Hugo_Symbol %in% c(driver_genes$Symbol, kdm$Approved.symbol)]
data <- data[order(data$pval, decreasing = FALSE), ]
pt.vs.rt$results <- data

# co-Oncoplot: LOY vs ROY
graphics.off() # Reset all devices
n <- 1
pdf(file = file.path(resPath, paste0("coOncoplot_", cancer, "_LOY_vs_ROY.pdf")), width = 12, height = 10)
coOncoplot(m1 = loy.maf, m2 = roy.maf, genes = combined_genes[1:30],
           m1Name = paste0(cancer, "-LOY"),
           m2Name = paste0(cancer, "-ROY"),
           clinicalFeatures1 = "Cohort",
           clinicalFeatures2 = "Cohort",
           removeNonMutated  = FALSE,
           anno_height       = 1.4,    # Extremely small to ensure vertical space
           #showSampleNames  = FALSE,
           #gene_mar         = 4,
           titleFontSize      = n * 1.5,
           annotationFontSize = n * 1.5,
           geneNamefont       = n,     # Tiny font for genes
           legendFontSize     = n * 1.5, # Tiny legends
           sortByAnnotation1  = TRUE,
           sortByAnnotation2  = TRUE)
dev.off()

# Companion forest plot (kept here for future panel; not part of Figure 1F)
n <- 0.8
pdf(file = file.path(resPath, paste0("forestPlot_", cancer, "_LOY_vs_ROY.pdf")), width = 6, height = 2.5)
forestPlot(mafCompareRes = pt.vs.rt, pVal = 0.05,
           geneFontSize = n, titleSize = n, lineWidth = 2)
dev.off()
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Session info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
